description: The design, analysis, and implementation of algorithms for solving mathematical problems approximately on computers. Covers error analysis, root finding, interpolation, numerical integration, linear systems, and numerical ODEs.

concepts.name: Floating-Point Arithmetic
concepts.description: Floating-point numbers: ±m × 2^e with mantissa m and exponent e. Machine epsilon ε: smallest number with 1+ε≠1. IEEE 754 standard: 32-bit (single), 64-bit (double). Catastrophic cancellation, overflow, underflow.
references: Higham, Accuracy and Stability of Numerical Algorithms, Ch.2; Trefethen & Bau, Numerical Linear Algebra, Lecture 13

concepts.name: Error Analysis
concepts.description: Absolute error: |x̂−x|. Relative error: |x̂−x|/|x|. Sources: round-off (finite precision), truncation (approximation of infinite process). Forward/backward error analysis; condition number κ(A) measures problem sensitivity.
references: Burden & Faires, Numerical Analysis, Ch.1; Higham, Accuracy and Stability, Ch.1

concepts.name: Root-Finding Methods
concepts.description: Bisection: O(log(1/ε)) iterations, guaranteed. Newton's method: quadratic convergence near a root (but requires f′). Secant: superlinear, no derivatives. Brent's method combines bisection + secant for robustness.
references: Burden & Faires, Numerical Analysis, Ch.2; Trefethen & Bau, Numerical Linear Algebra

concepts.name: Newton's Method
concepts.description: xₙ₊₁ = xₙ − f(xₙ)/f′(xₙ). Quadratic convergence near simple roots: error at step n+1 is O((error at step n)²). Fails or converges slowly near multiple roots or saddle points. Generalizes to systems: xₙ₊₁ = xₙ − [J(xₙ)]⁻¹F(xₙ).

concepts.name: Polynomial Interpolation
concepts.description: Given n+1 data points, the unique interpolating polynomial of degree ≤n. Lagrange form: L(x)=∑yᵢ∏_{j≠i}(x−xⱼ)/(xᵢ−xⱼ). Newton form: nested multiplication. Runge's phenomenon: high-degree polynomials oscillate at edges.

concepts.name: Spline Interpolation
concepts.description: Piecewise polynomial curves with continuity conditions across breakpoints. Cubic splines: C²; natural splines add boundary conditions. B-splines form a basis for spline spaces and are numerically stable. Avoid Runge's phenomenon.
references: Burden & Faires, Numerical Analysis, §3.4; de Boor, A Practical Guide to Splines

concepts.name: Numerical Differentiation
concepts.description: Forward: f′(x) ≈ (f(x+h)−f(x))/h, error O(h). Centered: (f(x+h)−f(x−h))/(2h), error O(h²). Richardson extrapolation improves accuracy. Trade-off: small h reduces truncation error but increases round-off.

concepts.name: Numerical Integration
concepts.description: Trapezoidal rule: O(h²) per panel. Simpson's rule: O(h⁴). Composite rules for [a,b]. Gaussian quadrature: n-point rule exact for degree ≤2n−1 polynomials; uses Legendre polynomial nodes and weights. Adaptive methods control error automatically.

concepts.name: LU Factorization (Numerical)
concepts.description: Partial pivoting ensures numerical stability: PA=LU. Operation count O(n³/3). Condition number κ(A)=|A||A⁻¹| governs error amplification: relative error ≤ κ(A) × relative residual.
references: Golub & Van Loan, Matrix Computations, Ch.3; Trefethen & Bau, Numerical Linear Algebra, Lecture 20

concepts.name: Cholesky Factorization (Numerical)
concepts.description: For symmetric positive definite A: A=LLᵀ, computed in O(n³/6) flops — half the cost of LU. Backward stable. Test for positive definiteness: Cholesky fails iff A is not positive definite.

concepts.name: Iterative Methods for Linear Systems
concepts.description: Jacobi and Gauss-Seidel: simple splitting methods; converge for strictly diagonally dominant A. Conjugate gradient: optimal for SPD systems, O(√κ) iterations. GMRES for general A. Preconditioning reduces condition number.
references: Golub & Van Loan, Matrix Computations, Ch.11; Saad, Iterative Methods for Sparse Linear Systems

concepts.name: QR Algorithm
concepts.description: Iteratively computes eigenvalues: A₀=A, A_{k+1}=RₖQₖ (QR decomposition of Aₖ). Converges to quasi-upper triangular form with eigenvalues on diagonal. With shifts, converges cubically for symmetric A. Foundation of LAPACK's dgeev.
references: Golub & Van Loan, Matrix Computations, Ch.8; Trefethen & Bau, Numerical Linear Algebra, Lecture 28

concepts.name: SVD (Numerical)
concepts.description: Computed via bidiagonalization (Golub-Reinsch): first reduce A to bidiagonal B by Householder reflectors, then apply QR-like iteration. Backward stable; gives singular values to machine precision. Cost O(mn²) for m≥n.
references: Golub & Van Loan, Matrix Computations, Ch.8.6

concepts.name: Euler's Method (Numerical ODE)
concepts.description: yₙ₊₁ = yₙ + hf(tₙ,yₙ). Global error O(h); local truncation error O(h²). Simple but often too inaccurate; stiff equations require implicit Euler (yₙ₊₁ = yₙ + hf(tₙ₊₁,yₙ₊₁)) for stability.

concepts.name: Runge-Kutta Methods
concepts.description: RK4: classic fourth-order method; evaluates f at four points per step; global error O(h⁴). Embedded pairs (Dormand-Prince, Fehlberg) estimate error and control step size adaptively. Butcher tableau encodes all RK methods.
references: Burden & Faires, Numerical Analysis, §5.2; Hairer, Nørsett & Wanner, Solving ODEs I

concepts.name: Stiff ODEs and Implicit Methods
concepts.description: Stiff ODEs have widely separated eigenvalues; explicit methods need tiny steps. Implicit Euler (backward Euler), trapezoidal rule, and BDF methods are A-stable or stiffly stable. Require solving nonlinear equations at each step.
references: Hairer & Wanner, Solving ODEs II: Stiff and Differential-Algebraic Problems
